35.kj: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, [idiom] first, [idiom] old, [phrase] owl, town, village.

35.gl: properly, to sigh, i.e. breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. (in a favorable sense) to pity, console or (reflexively) rue; or (unfavorably) to avenge (oneself)

36.gl: a eunuch; by implication, valet (especially of the female apartments), and thus, a minister of state
36.dv: or סָרִס; from an unused root meaning to castrate;

36.gl: a head person (of any rank or class)
36.kj: captain (that had rule), chief (captain), general, governor, keeper, lord,(-task-)master, prince(-ipal), ruler, steward.

36.gl: properly, a butcher; hence, a lifeguardsman (because he was acting as an executioner); also a cook (usually slaughtering the animal for food)
to sell, literally (as merchandise, a daughter in marriage, into slavery), or figuratively (to surrender)
Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt
